title: Catalepsy test in rats
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"frg7i","text":"Protocol for performing the catalepsy bar test in rats. This test evaluates muscular rigidity, and can be used in rat models with motor deficits, such as models of Parkison's disease.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Bring cages to the behavioral room for at least 01:00:00 prior to the test for habituation
Place both forepaws of the rat on an horizontal bar elevated so that the rat is in an half rearing position
Measure the descent latency, defined as the time it takes for both forepaws to be removed from the bar
Repeat the test three times, with a brief rest in between
creator: Eduard Bentea
